title: Doe manifest-shape intra-block probe-point map
description: Schema for probe-map configs consumed by `bench/tools/run_intra_block_probe_receipt.py` (single-block parity step). Maps each of the four TSIR boundary probe points (`post_rmsnorm`, `post_qkv`, `post_attn`, `post_ffn`) to the per-kernel dispatch output that lands at that boundary. The recorder uses the map to select which `.npy` snapshots from the per-kernel dispatch scratch dir constitute the four-probe set for one layer, hashes them, and emits a receipt the frozen-Doppler fixture comparator can score against.

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of this schema. Currently 1.

artifactKind.description: Receipt class identifier; used by aggregation tooling.

rationale.description: Free-form note documenting how the kernel→probe binding was decided (which kernel emits the activation at each boundary, which output symbol carries it).

probePoints.description: Map from one of the four canonical probe points to the kernel + output symbol that materializes that boundary's activation tensor.

kernel.description: compileTargets[].name — the kernel whose output tensor is the probe-point activation.

outputSymbol.description: Symbol name in the kernel's pe_program.metadata.json exports whose post-dispatch buffer is the probe activation.

note.description: Optional free-form note (e.g. why this kernel/symbol pair was chosen for this probe point).
